---
title: "hierarchal_clustering"
format: html
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r Info}
# Analysis adapted from: https://ngs101.com/how-to-cluster-rna-seq-data-to-uncover-gene-expression-patterns-hierarchical-and-k-means-methods-for-absolute-beginners
```

```{r setup}
library(here)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(ggrepel)
library(tidyverse)
library(limma)
library(DESeq2)
library(pheatmap)
library(ggforce)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(enrichplot)
library(clusterProfiler)
library(org.Mm.eg.db)
library(stringr)
library(ComplexHeatmap)
library(circlize)
library(mclust)
library(reshape2)

here()

source("~/Documents/RProjects/2026_AAV-TuD_2wDIOCohort/R/functions.R")
```

```{r Import data}
meta <- import_meta_data("meta/metadata.csv")

raw_counts_matrix <- import_raw_counts_matrix("data-raw/salmon.merged.gene_counts.tsv", meta)


# Make a gene list using annotations from raw_counts_matrix
genes_annot <- raw_counts_matrix |>
  rownames_to_column(var = "gene_id") |>
  dplyr::select("gene_id", "gene_name")


# Get mouse annotations
# Load libraries
library(AnnotationHub)
library(ensembldb)

# Connect to AnnotationHub
ah <- AnnotationHub()
ah

# Query AnnotationHub
mouse_ens <- query(ah, c("Mus musculus", "EnsDb", "GRCm39"))
mouse_ens

# Extract annotations of interest (as of Apr 2026: GRCm39, release 113)
# If you want GRCm38, use release 102.
mouse_ens <- mouse_ens[["AH116909"]] # Ensembl release 112 for Mus Musculus; release 113 not available yet.

# Extract gene-level information
genes(mouse_ens, return.type = "data.frame")

# Extract transcript-level information
transcripts(mouse_ens, return.type = "data.frame")

# Extract exon-level information
exons(mouse_ens, return.type = "data.frame")

# Create a gene-level dataframe based on the genes from the raw_counts matrix
annotations_ahb <- genes(mouse_ens, return.type = "data.frame") |>
  dplyr::select(gene_id, gene_name, entrezid, gene_biotype, description) |>
  dplyr::filter(gene_id %in% genes_annot$gene_id)
```

```{r Data filtering and cleanup}
#### Filter iWAT data
iWAT_meta <- meta |>
  dplyr::filter(tissue == "iWAT")

iWAT_samples <- meta |>
  dplyr::filter(tissue == "iWAT") |>
  dplyr::pull(sample_name)

iWAT_raw_counts <- raw_counts_matrix |>
  dplyr::select(gene_name, dplyr::all_of(iWAT_samples)) |>
  dplyr::select(-"gene_name") |>
  base::round()

all(colnames(iWAT_raw_counts) %in% rownames(iWAT_meta))
all(colnames(iWAT_raw_counts) == rownames(iWAT_meta))


#### Choose only samples with confirmed miR-335 KD in TM assay
iWAT_meta_clean <- iWAT_meta |>
  dplyr::filter(mouse_ID %in% c(
    "JWK-0395",
    "JWK-0396",
    "JWK-0397",
    "JWK-0407",
    "JWK-0373",
    "JWK-0400",
    "JWK-0401",
    "JWK-0402",
    "JWK-0377",
    "JWK-0378"
  ))

iWAT_samples <- iWAT_meta_clean |>
  dplyr::filter(tissue == "iWAT") |>
  dplyr::pull(sample_name)

iWAT_raw_counts <- raw_counts_matrix |>
  dplyr::select(gene_name, dplyr::all_of(iWAT_samples)) |>
  dplyr::select(-"gene_name") |>
  base::round()


#### DEseq normalized counts
all(colnames(iWAT_raw_counts) %in% rownames(iWAT_meta_clean))
all(colnames(iWAT_raw_counts) == rownames(iWAT_meta_clean))
```

```{r DEseq}
## Create DESeq2Dataset object
dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(
  countData = iWAT_raw_counts,
  colData = iWAT_meta_clean,
  design = ~group
)

# Perform the DE analysis
dds <- DESeq(dds)

resultsNames(dds)
# Make sure that the control is at the beginning of the levels
dds$group

# Extract results table
contrast <- c("group", "miR-335-TuD_6w", "ctrl-TuD_6w")
res_table <- results(dds, contrast = contrast, alpha = 0.05) # FDR > 0.05

# Filter genes by zero expression
res_table |>
  data.frame() |>
  dplyr::filter(baseMean == 0)

# Filter genes that have an extreme outlier
res_table |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  dplyr::filter(
    is.na(pvalue),
    is.na(padj),
    baseMean > 0
  )

# Filter genes below the low mean threshold
res_table |>
  data.frame() |>
  dplyr::filter(
    !is.na(pvalue),
    is.na(padj),
    baseMean > 0
  )

# LFC shrinking
# Save the unshrunken results to compare
res_table_unshrunken <- res_table

# Apply fold change shrinkage
res_table <- lfcShrink(dds, coef = "group_miR.335.TuD_6w_vs_ctrl.TuD_6w", type = "apeglm")
# In the unshrunken fold change values plotted, you can see the abundance of scatter for the the lowly expressed genes.
# That is, many of the low expressors exhibit very high fold changes.
# After shrinkage, we see the fold changes are much smaller estimates.

# Check how many DEGs were retreived
summary(res_table, alpha = 0.05) # padj/FDR < 0.05

# Compare with unshrunken
summary(res_table_unshrunken, alpha = 0.05)

# MA plot
plotMA(res_table, ylim = c(-2, 2))
plotMA(res_table_unshrunken, ylim = c(-2, 2))

padj.cutoff <- 0.05

# Create a tibble of results and add back gene ids
res_table_tb <- res_table |>
  data.frame() |>
  rownames_to_column(var = "gene_id") |>
  as_tibble()

# Add gene_name from annotations table
res_table_tb <- res_table_tb |>
  inner_join(annotations_ahb, by = "gene_id")

# Subset the tibble to keep only significant genes (padj<0.05)
sig_kd <- res_table_tb |>
  dplyr::filter(padj < padj.cutoff)

sig_kd

# Optional: Filter upregulated genes
upregulated_genes <- res_table |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  tibble::rownames_to_column("gene_id") |>
  dplyr::filter(log2FoldChange > 0, padj < 0.05) |>
  dplyr::pull(gene_id)
```


```{r Hierarchal clustering}
#-----------------------------------------------
# STEP 1: Normalize count data using DESeq2
#-----------------------------------------------

dds <- estimateSizeFactors(dds)

# Apply variance stabilizing transformation (VST)
vst_data <- vst(dds, blind = TRUE)
normalized_counts <- assay(vst_data)

normalized_counts <- normalized_counts |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  rownames_to_column(var = "gene_id") |> # Transfer the row names to a new column called "gene_id"
  as_tibble() |>
  left_join(
    raw_counts_matrix |>
      rownames_to_column(var = "gene_id") |>
      dplyr::select(gene_id, gene_name),
    by = "gene_id"
  )

#-----------------------------------------------
# STEP 2: Scale gene expression data
#-----------------------------------------------
# Create annotation dataframe for samples
annotation_col <- data.frame(
  group = colData(dds)$group,
  row.names = rownames(colData(dds))
)

# Build heatmap matrix
sig_gene_names <- sig_kd |>
  dplyr::select("gene_id") |>
  pull()

heatmap_data <- assay(vst_data) |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  tibble::rownames_to_column("gene_id") |>
  dplyr::filter(gene_id %in% sig_gene_names) |>
  tibble::column_to_rownames("gene_id")

# Build heatmap matrix - only for upregulated genes
heatmap_data <- assay(vst_data) |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  tibble::rownames_to_column("gene_id") |>
  dplyr::filter(gene_id %in% upregulated_genes) |>
  tibble::column_to_rownames("gene_id")

# Scale DEG expression data (row-wise/gene-wise scaling)
# Each gene is centered to mean=0 and scaled to sd=1
deg_scaled <- t(scale(t(heatmap_data)))

#-----------------------------------------------
# STEP 3: Hierarchal clustering of DEGs
#-----------------------------------------------

# Perform hierarchical clustering on DEGs (genes as rows)
# Distance: 1 - Pearson correlation (emphasizes pattern similarity)
# Linkage: complete (creates compact, well-separated clusters)
deg_gene_dist <- as.dist(1 - cor(t(deg_scaled), method = "pearson")) # Correlation-based distance for genes: Focuses on expression patterns rather than magnitudes. Genes with similar patterns (both increasing or decreaing) will cluster together.
deg_gene_hclust <- hclust(deg_gene_dist, method = "complete")

# Perform hierarchical clustering on samples (samples as columns)
# Distance: Euclidean (captures overall expression differences)
deg_sample_dist <- dist(t(deg_scaled), method = "euclidean") # Euclidean distance for samples: Captures overall expression differences between samples.
deg_sample_hclust <- hclust(deg_sample_dist, method = "complete")

# Generate heatmap with hierarchical clustering
pheatmap(
  deg_scaled,
  cluster_rows = deg_gene_hclust,
  cluster_cols = deg_sample_hclust,
  annotation_col = annotation_col,
  show_rownames = FALSE,
  show_colnames = TRUE,
  color = colorRampPalette(c("blue", "white", "red"))(100),
  main = "Hierarchical Clustering: Differentially Expressed Genes",
  fontsize = 10,
  filename = "hierarchical_clustering_DEGs.png",
  width = 10,
  height = 8
)

#-----------------------------------------------
# STEP 4: Extract gene clusters from dendrogram
#-----------------------------------------------

# Cut the dendrogram to create discrete clusters
n_clusters <- 2 # Separates genes upregulated in one condition, from those downregulated.
gene_clusters <- cutree(deg_gene_hclust, k = n_clusters)

# Create a dataframe showing cluster assignments
cluster_assignments <- data.frame(
  Gene = names(gene_clusters),
  Cluster = gene_clusters
)

# Count genes in each cluster
print(table(gene_clusters))


#-----------------------------------------------
# STEP 5: Create heatmap with cluster gaps
#-----------------------------------------------

# Reorder genes by cluster assignment
gene_order <- order(gene_clusters)
deg_scaled_ordered <- deg_scaled[gene_order, ]
cluster_ordered <- gene_clusters[gene_order]

# Create cluster annotation for genes
cluster_annotation <- data.frame(
  Cluster = factor(cluster_ordered),
  row.names = rownames(deg_scaled_ordered)
)

# Calculate gap positions (where clusters change)
gap_positions <- which(diff(cluster_ordered) != 0)

# Create annotated heatmap with gaps between clusters
pheatmap(
  deg_scaled_ordered,
  cluster_rows = FALSE, # Already ordered by cluster
  cluster_cols = deg_sample_hclust,
  annotation_row = cluster_annotation,
  annotation_col = annotation_col,
  gaps_row = gap_positions, # Add visual gaps between clusters
  show_rownames = FALSE,
  show_colnames = TRUE,
  color = colorRampPalette(c("blue", "white", "red"))(100),
  main = "DEG Hierarchical Clusters (Separated by Gaps)",
  fontsize = 10,
  filename = "hierarchical_clustering_DEGs_gapped.png",
  width = 10,
  height = 8
)
```

```{r K-means clustering}
#-----------------------------------------------
# STEP 6: Determine optimal K using elbow method
#-----------------------------------------------

# Calculate within-cluster sum of squares (WSS) for different K values
# WSS measures cluster compactness - lower values indicate tighter clusters
wss <- sapply(1:15, function(k) {
  kmeans(deg_scaled, centers = k, nstart = 25)$tot.withinss
})

# Create data frame for plotting
elbow_data <- data.frame(
  K = 1:15,
  WSS = wss
)

# Plot the elbow curve
ggplot(elbow_data, aes(x = K, y = WSS)) +
  geom_line(color = "steelblue", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(color = "steelblue", size = 3) +
  labs(
    title = "Elbow Method for Optimal K",
    x = "Number of Clusters (K)",
    y = "Within-Cluster Sum of Squares"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 10)
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:15)

# ggsave("elbow_plot.png", width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)

# Interpretation:  Look for the “elbow” – the point where the curve transitions from steep to shallow. Adding clusters beyond the elbow provides diminishing improvements in compactness.
# For example, if WSS drops sharply from K=1 to K=3 then levels off, K=3 is likely optimal.

#-----------------------------------------------
# STEP 7: Silhouette analysis for K selection
#-----------------------------------------------
# The silhouette method provides an alternative, often more reliable approach to selecting optimal K.

# Load cluster package for silhouette calculation
library(cluster)

# Function to calculate average silhouette width
calculate_silhouette <- function(data, max_k = 15) {
  sil_width <- sapply(2:max_k, function(k) {
    km <- kmeans(data, centers = k, nstart = 25)
    ss <- silhouette(km$cluster, dist(data))
    mean(ss[, 3]) # Average silhouette width
  })

  data.frame(
    K = 2:max_k,
    Silhouette = sil_width
  )
}

# Calculate silhouette scores for K from 2 to 15
sil_data <- calculate_silhouette(deg_scaled)

# Plot silhouette scores
ggplot(sil_data, aes(x = K, y = Silhouette)) +
  geom_line(color = "darkgreen", size = 1) +
  geom_point(color = "darkgreen", size = 3) +
  labs(
    title = "Silhouette Analysis for Optimal K",
    x = "Number of Clusters (K)",
    y = "Average Silhouette Width"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 10)
  )

# Identify optimal K (K with maximum silhouette width)
optimal_k <- sil_data$K[which.max(sil_data$Silhouette)]
cat("Optimal K based on silhouette analysis:", optimal_k, "\n")

# Interpretation: Values range from -1 to 1.
# Higher values indicate better defined clusters.
# Values > 0.5 suggest strong cluster structure
# Values < 0.25 suggest weak or artificial clustering
# Select K with the maximum average silhouette width.

#-----------------------------------------------
# STEP 8: K-means clustering of DEGs
#-----------------------------------------------

# Perform K-means clustering on DEGs
# nstart = 25 runs algorithm 25 times with different random starts
# and returns the best result (lowest within-cluster SS)
set.seed(123) # Always run! For reproducibility
kmeans_deg <- kmeans(deg_scaled, centers = optimal_k, nstart = 25)

# Extract cluster assignments
deg_kmeans_clusters <- kmeans_deg$cluster

# Order genes by cluster for visualization
deg_cluster_order <- order(deg_kmeans_clusters)
deg_scaled_ordered_km <- deg_scaled[deg_cluster_order, ]
deg_clusters_ordered <- deg_kmeans_clusters[deg_cluster_order]

# Calculate gap positions between clusters
deg_gap_positions <- which(diff(deg_clusters_ordered) != 0)

# Custom cluster colors
cluster_ids <- levels(factor(deg_clusters_ordered)) # Get cluster ids
# Define colors (adjust number as needed)
cluster_colors <- setNames(
  c("#9F8BA2", "#AE8679")[seq_along(cluster_ids)],
  cluster_ids
)

# Create annotated heatmap with cluster separation
pheatmap(
  deg_scaled_ordered_km,
  name = "Row Z-score",
  cluster_rows = FALSE, # Already ordered by K-means cluster
  cluster_cols = deg_sample_hclust,
  annotation_row = data.frame(
    Cluster = factor(deg_clusters_ordered),
    row.names = rownames(deg_scaled_ordered_km)
  ),
  annotation_col = annotation_col,
  annotation_colors = list(
    group = c(
      "ctrl-TuD_6w" = "#54494B",
      "miR-335-TuD_6w" = "#E5C689"
    ),
    Cluster = cluster_colors
  ),
  gaps_row = deg_gap_positions, # Visual separation between clusters
  show_rownames = FALSE,
  show_colnames = TRUE,
  color = colorRampPalette(c("#B4C7D6", "white", "#A94642"))(100),
  main = "K-means Clustering: Differentially Expressed Genes",
  fontsize = 10,
)

# Save cluster assignments for downstream analysis
deg_cluster_assignments <- data.frame(
  Gene = names(deg_kmeans_clusters),
  Cluster = deg_kmeans_clusters
)

write.csv(deg_cluster_assignments,
  "kmeans_DEG_cluster_assignments.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)
```

```{r Compare clustering methods}
# Create comparison dataframe
comparison_df <- data.frame(
  Gene = rownames(deg_scaled),
  Hierarchical = gene_clusters,
  Kmeans = deg_kmeans_clusters
)

# Calculate Adjusted Rand Index (ARI)
# ARI measures agreement between two clustering results
# Values range from -1 to 1 (1 = perfect agreement, 0 = random)
ari <- adjustedRandIndex(gene_clusters, deg_kmeans_clusters)
cat("Adjusted Rand Index between methods:", round(ari, 3), "\n")
# Adjusted Rand Index between methods: 0.904

# Create confusion matrix
# Shows how genes are distributed across clusters in each method
confusion <- table(
  Hierarchical = gene_clusters,
  Kmeans = deg_kmeans_clusters
)
print(confusion)

# Visualize the comparison
confusion_melt <- melt(confusion)
confusion_melt$Hierarchical <- as.factor(confusion_melt$Hierarchical)
confusion_melt$Kmeans <- as.factor(confusion_melt$Kmeans)

ggplot(confusion_melt, aes(x = Kmeans, y = Hierarchical, fill = value)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_text(aes(label = value), color = "white", size = 4) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "lightblue", high = "darkblue") +
  labs(
    title = "Comparison of Hierarchical vs K-means Clustering",
    x = "K-means Cluster",
    y = "Hierarchical Cluster",
    fill = "Gene Count"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12)
  )

ggsave("clustering_method_comparison.png", width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)
```

```{r GSEA on clusters}
# Get gene list
df <- deg_cluster_assignments |>
  inner_join(res_table_tb, by = c("Gene" = "gene_id"))

gene_list <- df$log2FoldChange
names(gene_list) <- df$gene_name
gene_list <- sort(gene_list, decreasing = TRUE)


# Run GSEA per cluster
clusters <- unique(df$Cluster)

gsea_results <- list()

for (cl in clusters) {
  message("Running cluster: ", cl)

  cluster_genes <- df %>%
    dplyr::filter(Cluster == cl) %>%
    pull(gene_name)

  # Optional: highlight cluster genes by weighting (NOT required for fgsea)
  fg <- fgsea(
    pathways = pathways,
    stats = gene_list,
    nperm = 10000,
    minSize = 10,
    maxSize = 500
  )

  fg <- fg %>% arrange(padj)

  gsea_results[[paste0("cluster_", cl)]] <- fg
}

head(gsea_results$cluster_1, 10)
head(gsea_results$cluster_2, 10)

# Plot the reuslts
plot_df <- bind_rows(gsea_results, .id = "Cluster") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Cluster = gsub("cluster_", "", Cluster))

plot_df_sig <- plot_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(padj < 0.05) %>%
  arrange(Cluster, padj)


ggplot(plot_df_sig, aes(x = NES, y = reorder(pathway, NES))) +
  geom_point(aes(size = size, color = padj)) +
  facet_wrap(~Cluster, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "red", high = "blue") +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    x = "Normalized Enrichment Score (NES)",
    y = "Pathway",
    color = "padj",
    size = "Gene set size"
  )
```

```{r ORA on clusters}
clusters <- unique(df$Cluster)

# Get gene list
res_table_df <- res_table |> 
  data.frame() |>
  rownames_to_column(var = "gene_id")

df <- deg_cluster_assignments %>%
  inner_join(res_table_df, by = c("Gene" = "gene_id")) %>%
  inner_join(annotations_ahb, by = c("Gene" = "gene_id")) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(entrezid))

ora_results <- list()

for (cl in clusters) {
  cluster_genes <- df %>%
    dplyr::filter(Cluster == cl) %>%
    pull(entrezid) %>%
    unique()

  ego <- enrichGO(
    gene          = cluster_genes,
    OrgDb         = org.Mm.eg.db,
    keyType       = "ENTREZID",
    ont           = "BP",
    pAdjustMethod = "BH",
    pvalueCutoff  = 0.05,
    qvalueCutoff  = 0.2,
    universe      = df$entrezid
  )

  ora_results[[paste0("cluster_", cl)]] <- ego
}

head(ora_results$cluster_1)
head(ora_results$cluster_2)
head(ora_results$cluster_3)

dotplot(ora_results$cluster_1, showCategory = 20)
dotplot(ora_results$cluster_2, showCategory = 20)

library(patchwork)

plots <- lapply(names(ora_results), function(nm) {
  dotplot(ora_results[[nm]], showCategory = 20) +

    ggtitle(nm)
})

wrap_plots(plots)


# Updated figures

#-----------------------
# Extract ORA results
#-----------------------
df_plot_1 <- ora_results$cluster_1@result

#-----------------------
# Select top 20 pathways
#-----------------------
df_plot_top <- df_plot_1 %>%
  arrange(p.adjust, desc(Count)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 20)

#-----------------------
# Convvert GeneRatio to numeric
#-----------------------
df_plot_top <- df_plot_top %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    GeneRatio_num = sapply(GeneRatio, function(x) {
      eval(parse(text = x))
    })
  )

#-----------------------
# Wrap pathway names for compact figure
#-----------------------
df_plot_top$Description <- str_wrap(df_plot_top$Description, width = 35)

df_plot_top <- df_plot_top %>%
  mutate(
    Description = factor(
      Description,
      levels = Description[order(GeneRatio_num)]
    )
  )

ggplot(df_plot_top, aes(
  x = GeneRatio_num,
  y = Description
)) +
  geom_point(aes(
    size = Count,
    color = p.adjust
  )) +
  scale_color_gradient(
    low = "#54494B",
    high = "#A94642"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 10) +
  labs(
    x = "Gene Ratio",
    y = NULL,
    color = "Adj. p-value",
    size = "Gene Count"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 8),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 9),
    plot.margin = margin(5, 5, 5, 5)
  )


# Get genes in a specific pathway
head(ora_results$cluster_1@result$Description)
pathway_name <- "purine ribonucleotide metabolic process"

genes_in_pathway <- ora_results$cluster_1@result %>%
  dplyr::filter(Description == pathway_name) %>%
  pull(geneID)

genes_in_pathway <- strsplit(genes_in_pathway, "/")[[1]]

genes_df <- annotations_ahb %>%
  dplyr::filter(entrezid %in% genes_in_pathway)

pathway_genes <- df %>% dplyr::filter(Gene %in% genes_df$gene_id) %>%
  group_by(Cluster)

# Save gene list
# write_csv(pathway_genes, "results/clust1_ORA_pathway_genes.csv")
```
